spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (fetch(, SharedWorker) with runtime script (~3443 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 4 buttons, 1 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (after, analytics, automatically, backed, close, connectivity, enqueued, extended, flush, keeps, lifetime, message, messa).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("This update adds a new option, `extendedLifetime: true`, to the `SharedWorker` constructor. This new option requests that the shared worker be kept alive even after all current clients have unloaded. This allows pages to perform asynchronou") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/commit/9c009049e4fa9dba638ef68ca502b781082bbb68 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3443 chars of script across 4 button(s) and 1 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 1 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
